title: aita for asking my husband to see a movie on a different day than he wants to?
text: this might be a long post, i apologize in advance. last night, my (28f) husband (29m) said he wants to go to the movies next wednesday. he works 12 hours and a 3/2/2 schedule, so next week his days off are wednesday and thursday. the week after, he’ll only work those days and have off m, t, and the weekend starting friday. i work from home doing customer service on the phone all day, monday - friday and every other saturday. we have three kids (9m, 7f, and a 12 week old baby girl). the 9 and 7 year old of course are at school during the day, but on the days that my husband works i’m taking care of baby girl while working. she’s quietest when she’s being held (and i need her to be quiet of course because i’m on the phone), so i wear her my entire 8 hour work day. i’ve explained to my husband how it’s stressful to work and take care of her because she’s still obviously not going to be quiet the entire day, and i’m having to calm her/feed her/change diapers all while on the phone, and use my breaks to either feed her or try to keep up with the house.  so back to last night. he said he wants to go to the movies next wednesday. i work next wednesday, so i said i would rather i didn’t have to work and take care of a baby more than is absolutely necessary. i asked him to go on one of his sundays off because i’m guaranteed to be off so i would only have to worry about taking care of the house and the baby instead of trying to work too. he said sundays would be too busy for his preference so he would rather go during the week, and that it’s “only a couple hours, you act like it’s a big deal.” i explained that it is a big deal to me because that’s one of the days i consider a “break” because i only have to worry about work, and i didn’t understand the big deal about just going on a different day. he told me i’m selfish because i won’t let him take 2 hours to himself. i said he could, that i just want him to do it on a day that’s easier for me. not to mention, i don’t even sit down to breathe for the day until 10pm, and then i’m so exhausted that i just end up falling asleep. on my days off, i’m cleaning the house all day. i don’t get time at all, while he plays video games on his days off and does nothing in the house. so hearing him call me selfish was so hurtful. am i wrong for just asking him to go on a different day? we discussed it this morning and he still doesn’t understand where i’m coming from, so maybe i am making it a bigger deal than it is.

storyboard.content: My husband (29M) and I (28F) have three kids — ages 9, 7, and a 12-week-old baby girl. He works 12-hour shifts on a 3/2/2 schedule, and I work from home doing customer service on the phone, Monday through Friday and every other Saturday.

On the days my husband works, I’m taking care of the baby while working. She’s quietest when being held, so I wear her for my entire 8-hour work day, calming her, feeding her, and changing diapers all while on the phone.
My husband said he wants to go to the movies next Wednesday, which is one of his days off but a day I have to work.
I asked him to go on one of his Sundays off instead, since I’m guaranteed to be off that day and would only have to worry about the house and the baby rather than working too.
He said Sundays would be too busy for his preference and dismissed my concern, saying it’s “only a couple hours, you act like it’s a big deal.”
He told me I’m selfish because I won’t let him take 2 hours to himself.
I don’t even sit down to breathe until 10pm, and on my days off I’m cleaning the house all day. I don’t get any time to myself at all.
On his days off, he plays video games and does nothing in the house.
We discussed it again this morning and he still doesn’t understand where I’m coming from.
